features: Automated reports from Google Analytics + 2 ad platforms
Weekly scheduled PDF reports + basic dashboard
Quarterly strategy calls with our team

overall_wtp_ceiling.description: Average maximum price respondent willing to pay across all three tiers
overall_wtp_ceiling.confidence_interpretation: The typical agency in our survey would be willing to pay up to $X/year

elasticity_800_to_1200.interpretation: If acceptance drops 15% when price increases 50%, elasticity = -0.30 (inelastic/good)

elasticity_1200_to_1800.interpretation: If acceptance drops 20% when price increases 50%, elasticity = -0.40 (somewhat elastic)

optimal_price_point.rationale: Price point with highest yes_percent (adoption) or best elasticity tradeoff

feature_preference_analysis.feature_adoption_interpretation: Which features are table-stakes vs. nice-to-have by tier?

1200_assumption_validation.interpretation: If YES, $1,200 pricing assumption is VALIDATED. If NO, pivot to lower price.

revenue_impact_analysis.if_1200_confirmed: Can proceed with $1,200 ARPU; LTV calculations hold
revenue_impact_analysis.if_pivot_to_1000: Reduces annual revenue per user from $1,200 to $1,000; LTV down 17%; still viable if CAC < $333/user
revenue_impact_analysis.if_pivot_to_800: Reduces annual revenue per user by 33%; LTV down 33%; requires lower CAC or higher volume to maintain unit economics

tier_viability.starter_800_viable: Is 800 tier viable as entry point? (measure: >70% yes)
tier_viability.standard_1200_viable: Is 1200 tier viable as main offering? (measure: >60% yes)
tier_viability.premium_1800_viable: Is 1800 tier viable as premium option? (measure: >35% yes)

survey_limitations: Small sample size (n=8-10 expected); results not statistically significant for segments
Selection bias: only high-engagement prospects included; may skew toward price-insensitive buyers
Hypothetical pricing: respondents may not behave as they predict; actual conversion may differ
No competitive comparison: survey doesn't show competitor pricing, so WTP may be influenced by anchoring

confidence_levels.elasticity_calculation: Low (would need 30+ respondents for statistical significance)

primary_recommendation.rationale: Survey should reveal whether this is optimal

alternative_strategy.description: If <50% accept $1,200, consider value-based pricing tied to time savings ROI
alternative_strategy.rationale: Price by value delivered, not by feature tier

feature_prioritization.table_stakes_for_mvp: Features selected by >60% at Starter tier
feature_prioritization.premium_differentiators: Features selected by >50% at Premium tier
feature_prioritization.deprioritize: Features with <30% selection across all tiers

segmentation_strategy.by_size: Consider size-based pricing if large agencies (50+) have 30%+ higher WTP
segmentation_strategy.by_revenue: Consider revenue-based pricing if revenue cohorts show clear elasticity differences
segmentation_strategy.by_authority: Tailor sales messaging differently for decision-makers vs. influencers

next_steps_if_validated: Proceed to pilot customer acquisition with $1,200 pricing
Create 3-tier pricing page on marketing site
Develop sales playbook with tier-specific value propositions
Set up pricing analytics to track tier adoption and CAC by segment
next_steps_if_not_validated: Conduct follow-up interviews with 2-3 respondents to understand price sensitivity
Consider lower starting price ($999 or $1,095) to improve adoption
Test value-based pricing (e.g., $150/month for teams under 30 people)
Re-run survey in 2-4 weeks after product roadmap refinements to signal stronger value

executive_summary.business_impact: This pricing decision will directly impact LTV and unit economics; final recommendation requires data analysis
